key_drivers: The U.S. dollar is no longer a strong headwind: DXY is only slightly firmer on the day but remains softer over the monthly horizon, supporting non-USD FX and commodities.
Commodity leadership is concentrated in crude, where the latest EIA week still shows a draw and the supply backdrop remains fragile despite the absence of a fresh panic spike.
Equity signals are selective because narrowing credit spreads and strong SOX performance are offset by a hawkish-hold Fed, slowing GDPNow growth, and an intraday rebound in MOVE.
Policy divergence remains the clearest directional theme: the RBA is openly hawkish after the March hike, the BoJ remains in gradual normalization mode, and the ECB is broadly neutral.

stale_sources: GDPNow remains the April 9 official update at 1.3% for 2026:Q1 until the next scheduled refresh later on April 21.
FRED DFII10 and HY OAS observations reflect normal publication lag relative to the report timestamp.
World Gold Council flow detail is anchored to the April 8 publication covering March ETF outflows.
fallbacks_used: Fed stance used readable same-session reporting citing CME FedWatch because the page text did not expose the full probability table cleanly.
TradingView was used for DXY, SOX, MOVE, and copper direction where direct primary pages were less accessible or blocked.
BoJ normalization classification used the official release calendar plus accessible March 19 reporting corroboration.
Copper used a recent verified fallback note because the specified Investing.com page was blocked in this environment.
overnight_changes: SOX remained strong while MOVE bounced on the day, keeping NQ constructive but not high conviction.
VIX remained in the high teens rather than shifting into a true risk-off regime.
DXY stayed near recent lows despite a small daily gain.

imminent: Atlanta Fed GDPNow update on April 21, 2026
